The popularity of scented candles in recent years is explained by pandemic forced constant stay at home. Thus, the desire to decorate the home and make it comfortable. According to Grand View Research, the container-based segment dominated the market for scented candles and accounted for the largest revenue share of more than 56.0% in 2020. Researchers claim that scented candles have their great popularity because of their therapeutic and soothing effect, millennials believe it helps them to maintain positive vibrations and clear mindset.  As the candle industry continues to grow, candle brands are looking for ways to expand their reach and grow their market share. One way to do this is to develop a comprehensive expansion strategy. A well-crafted expansion strategy will help a candle brand to identify new markets, develop new products, and reach new customers.     A particularly difficult situation is when a company is new to the market and does not have an established customer base, a well-known  name  and large investments. It is in this position that the Fromm brand, which produces scented candles, is located. The company faces a difficult question: how to increase market share, increase brand awareness and the number of sales in the conditions of economic downturn and high competition. Currently, the company is facing a problem: the number of sales is not growing at the planned pace.     The object of the work is the Fromm company, and the subject is its growth strategy in the Russian market,  taking into account  the achievement of recognition abroad. The purpose of this work is to develop practical recommendations for Fromm's growth strategy.     The set goal will be achieved by solving the following tasks: -Analysis of the current business  model. -Defining the strategic goals of the company's founder, identify the mission,  vision  and  values. -Analysis of the company's resources, identification of sustainable advantages that contribute to the successful operation of companies in the  market. -Conducting an analysis of the external environment, which will identify opportunities for growth and threats that hinder  it.   -Conducting consumer research to identify their needs and potential demand for brand  products. -Conducting a comparative analysis of competitors to identify current trends and international  practices.  -Practical recommendations for improving the business model.     The work consists of four chapters, an  introduction,  and a conclusion. The first chapter examines the Fromm company, analyzes its business  model,  and identifies the problem under study. The second chapter is devoted to strategic planning: an in-depth interview with the founder is conducted to identify the direction of growth, and the company's sustainable resources are identified. In the third chapter, the market is explored - threats and opportunities for growth are identified using PESTEL analysis, the potential demand for products from a new audience segment is investigated, and international and Russian practices of expansion strategies are analyzed using benchmarking. The fourth chapter provides strategic recommendations for the expansion of the company.      While  writing the work, financial reports of leading companies in the industry, qualitative and quantitative data on competitors collected on their official websites and social networks, information from news portals were used. Surveys of the potential audience were also conducted to identify the demand for products. Part  1. Company characteristics and Managerial problem   1.1 Company description       Fromm Candles -  Russian  company, that produces fragranced candles. It is a small sized company, which was founded in  June  2022 in Saint Petersburg, Russia. Fromm’s main product line - art candles inspired by impressionism, on the label the famous painting is  depicted,  and the fragrance is selected by associations with that painting. For example, on the label Iris of Vincent Van Gogh, the fragrance contains notes of flowers.  The brand was founded with a limited  number  of investments, initially, it was supposed to sell candles online through Instagram, promoting with Instagram ads. Nevertheless, promotion on Instagram was banned and alternative sources of promotion and distribution had to be found.  Key partners:      Taking into account the unique positioning of the brand (art candles), Fromm found partners among Saint Petersburg shops, associated with art and culture -  Hermitage Museum  shop and Dom  Knigi  as the main b2b partners with a very high  pass ability  (about 10-15  thousands  of people per day)  of completely diverse types of customers. For Hermitage and Dom  Knigi  special collections were developed according to their requirement, candles that are sold through them are not possible to buy through the official website of the brand. The second category of partners - elite bookstores (Masters Bookstore and  KGallery ) with small  passability   ( about 100-200 people per day), which sells ART collection of the brand. The audience of these bookstores could be described as Gen X, Gen Y and Gen Z with average to high income, who appreciate art, books, culture. Another category of producers is suppliers of materials - the key partners here is Russian company  Greenwax  that sells fragrances at a reasonable price, but the versatility of the flavors is rather weak and in general the line is not so extensive. Most Russian manufacturers use this supplier as the most affordable in the conditions of sanctions imposed on Russia. The second supplier is  Makesy , an American  company that produces materials for various cosmetic and aromatic products, their fragrances are much more expensive at cost, but they have expensive perfume compositions and a wide range, they are more unique. However, logistics difficulties arise due to sanctions, which further increases their cost and instability in supplies occurs.  The key supplier also printed house “ Pomidor ”, which has special printing material for labels, called ‘ Verzhe ’, which has the rough surface, thanks to which the label looks crafty and aged and vaguely resembles a newspaper clipping. Inasmuch as there is no item of marketing expenses, generally barter transactions with micro-influencers are used for advertising. Key partners in this aspect - blog 15 moments,  mood. of.nastya .  Key activities: 1. Candle Making: The most important activity of the business is the manufacturing of candles. This includes melting wax, adding fragrance and color, pouring the wax into molds, and allowing it to cool and set. 2. Product Design and Development: Candle businesses need to constantly develop new products to keep up with changing consumer preferences. This involves researching current trends, experimenting with new fragrances and colors, and creating unique designs. 3. Marketing and Sales: A successful candle business needs to market its products effectively to reach a wider audience. This includes developing a brand identity, creating marketing campaigns, and building relationships with retailers and customers. 4. Inventory Management: Managing inventory is a crucial part, this involves forecasting demand, ordering raw materials and supplies, and ensuring that there is enough stock to meet customer orders. 5. Packaging and Shipping: Candles need to be packaged and shipped to customers or retailers. This involves selecting appropriate packaging materials, labeling the products, and arranging for shipping and delivery. 6. Customer Service: Providing excellent customer service is essential for building a loyal customer base. This includes responding to customer inquiries, addressing complaints, and providing support to retailers who sell the candles. Key resources: The company's key resources are its USP. According to  Roser   Ree , unique selling proposition must include following criteria: uniqueness - the USP must be unique and  different from competitors' offers; clarity - the USP should be easily understandable and memorable for consumers; value - USP should have value for consumers and solve their problems or meet their needs; provability - the USP must be supported by evidence or real examples. Fromm’s idea with art candles is innovative, as nobody before uses impressionists’ paintings for candles, despite the current trend on using art in the objects of daily use .  Despite the crowded candle market (due to the low entry threshold and the popularity of the craft), the brand manages to establish cooperation thanks to a unique idea that has not been encountered before. The second key resource of the company is the partnership with the Hermitage, the status of an official supplier creates additional value both for end consumers and for establishing business partnerships.  Value proposition: The unique value  proposition includes  several points:  Candles are made in St. Petersburg, which means it is a local brand. The advantages of locality of the brand of candles are that buyers can be confident in the quality of the product and support local manufacturers. In addition, it allows you to quickly respond to customer requests and preferences, which allows you to create products that better meet the needs of your customers. It also contributes to the development of the region's economy and the creation of new jobs. Candles are poured by hand. Making candles manually allows control of the quality of each candle and guarantees its safety and durability. Manual manufacturing also receives a lot of attention from consumers and is considered more environmentally friendly and valuable for many. It also allows making custom products according to individual requests. Soy wax is used for production instead of paraffin. Soy wax is biodegradable and does not contain petroleum products, which makes candles made from it more environmentally friendly and safe for health. It is important to note that such candles burn longer than candles made of paraffin or other materials. Moreover, soy wax has a lower melting point than other materials, which allows for better distribution of the aroma of the air, and while burning it does not emit toxic emissions that can be harmful to health. Cultural values. The brand popularizes art and integrates it into everyday life, which perfectly coincides with the image of St. Petersburg - the cultural capital.  The brand was invented in France, it was inspired by the French way of life, their desire for beauty,  sophistication,  and love of handicraft production. The French appreciate art and transfer it into their daily life, creating vessels using famous works of art, jewelry or even cosmetics - all this unites Fromm. The main color of the brand is blue, as a symbol of the Cote d'Azur, where the brand was invented. Blue also means peace and tranquility, which reflects the essence of the product.  Customer relationship: There is no structured and well-developed customer relationship system in the company, the only proposition is a discount for the first order. It is essential to develop a loyalty system and standards for building strong relationships. Channels : B2B channels - through Dom  Knigi , Hermitage,  Masters  Bookstore and  KGallery B2C channels - through official websites, Instagram. Customer segments: Young women: They are  interested in buying candles for use at home, at work, or as a gift for friends and family. They may also be interested in a variety of scents,  colors  and shapes of candles to choose a candle that matches their personal preferences and interior style, this audience may also be interested in environmentally friendly candles that are made of natural materials and do not contain harmful substances. That segment usually uses candles as a source of pleasant aroma, which helps to calm down and enjoy the space, and they also burn candles for creating atmosphere.  Art lovers: They appreciate beauty,  aesthetics  and creativity. They can be professional artists or just art lovers who want to enjoy beautiful paintings and get aesthetic pleasure. They may also be interested in visiting galleries, museums,  exhibitions  and auctions. This audience is buying for use as decorations at home, at work. They tend to buy art candles as a souvenir after exhibitions or courses. Cost structure: 1. Raw Materials: This includes the cost of wax, fragrance oils, colorants, wicks, and other materials needed to make the candles. 2. Labor: The cost of labor includes the wages paid to employees involved in the manufacturing, packaging, and shipping of the candles. 3. Overhead: This includes expenses such as rent, utilities, insurance, and other fixed costs. 4. Marketing and Advertising: This includes the cost of creating marketing materials, advertising campaigns, and promoting the candles through various channels.  However  there are 0 marketing cost, as all advertising and over expenses associated with sales of products the owner of the company does by herself. 5. Shipping and Handling: This includes the cost of packaging materials, shipping fees, and any other expenses associated with delivering the candles to customers or retailers. 6. Taxes and Fees: This includes taxes on sales in the amount of 4 percent on income from individuals and in the amount of 6 percent on income from legal entities. Revenue streams:      1. Direct Sales: This includes selling candles directly to customers  through  online  store, social media stores or word-of-mouth. 2. B2B sales: This involves selling candles  in  gift  shops (Hermitage shop and Dom  Knigi ) and book stores (Masters bookstore and  KGallery ) 3. Private Labeling: This involves creating custom candles for other businesses or individuals who want to sell candles under their own brand name. In the case of OOMPH (the legal successor of LUSH in Russia), the special collection for New Year was developed in partnership.  4. Workshops and Classes: Corporate master classes are held for residents of  Yasnaya   Polyana  art coworking, where participants can not only choose the fragrance they like, but also a sticker with a picture that fits this fragrance.        1.2 Research problem        According to the study, the HOME PERFUMERY MARKET – GROWTH, TRENDS AND FORECASTS (2023-2028) , the home fragrance market will grow by an average of 4.9% over the next 5 years, the study also mentions that the market is highly competitive and fragmented.   The average revenue of the competitors that have a close business model (NOTEM, flame, SP candle, BANKA home) is 160 000, 880 000, 5 000 000, 13 000 000 and 40 000 000 for 2017-2021 respectively, while brand Fromm has 250 000 for the 6 months of 2022 (June - December), which is significantly higher in comparison with the first year of competitors. Nevertheless, significant growth and expansion is noticed in the development of the competitors.  Due to the above factors, the company's request for this thesis is to develop a business expansion strategy that will allow it to profitably build up from numerous competitors, diversify customer segments  in order to  reduce risks and increase sales. According to the information from the representative of the company, the main revenue streams (90%) of the brand are from b2b partners -  Hermitage Museum  and Dom  Knigi , which sells special collections; the b2c sales of the main collection are only 10% of the overall brand revenue. The request from the company is to increase the overall  number  of sales, paying special attention to b2c  sales. The  aim of the consultant project is to develop expansion strategy, analyze consumer preferences and needs, and raise brand awareness among target audiences  including  foreign  buyers who visit St. Petersburg with the aim of further business development outside the country. The representative of the brand wants to raise sales through official websites and partners; own offline shops are not considered  at the moment  due to the limited amount of money to invest available.  1.3 Research design and theoretical background     The aim of the project is to develop the expansion strategy of the company. To define objectives, it is essential to analyze a variety of expansion strategies and choose appropriate ones as the basis for this work.  Throughout the history of business development, various authors have proposed a variety of strategies for expanding companies. It is essential to look at the main types of strategies for business expansion, their authors, how they can be applied and choose the appropriate one for the further analysis. Nevertheless, it should be noted that the company considered in this paper belongs to the small business segment, therefore it is necessary to consider  first of all  expansion strategies suitable for small businesses. What is the difference between a small business and a large one? The scale and availability of resources, therefore, they should use more limited and effective expansion strategies, such as increasing sales of existing goods or services, expanding the geographical service area and adding new products or services related to the core business of the company. At the same time, large businesses can afford such strategies as mergers and acquisitions, vertical integration. According to  Robert  Swaim   there are two dimensions for business growth. One is measurement of organic growth: increase in sales revenue due to sales and marketing efforts, including through price increases, and innovative efforts of the firm to develop new products or services, the second one is measurement of external growth: increase in sales revenue  as a result of  mergers and acquisitions (M&A). Horizontal acquisitions imply geographical expansion or the acquisition of new products that complement the existing product line of the company. Regressive vertical acquisitions imply the purchase of sources of supply, and progressive vertical acquisitions — the acquisition of a part of the marketing channel  in order to  reduce sales and marketing costs and get closer to customers. Strategic alliances, formal and informal, also provide opportunities for growth. Withdrawal of capital investment provides a short-term increase in profit  as a result of  the sale of this business unit, but also leads to a decrease in sales of products of this business unit.     Peter Drucker is considered one of the most influential thinkers in the field of management of the XX century, he was the first who began to study management as a science and developed many theories and principles that became the basis of modern management.  In his book  The Practice of Management   Peter Drucker proposes a strategy of innovation as the way of growth for the company. It assumes that innovations should be an integral part of the  company's business strategy, and that they should be aimed at meeting customer needs. The main principle of this strategy is customer orientation. The key meaning of this theory is that a company must understand the needs and desires of its customers  in order to  create appropriate services or products. This requires continuous research of the market and its consumers, the study of their habits and behavior. An important element of Peter Drucker's innovation strategy is a systematic approach to innovation. He believed that companies should have a process of developing and implementing new products and services, which would include all stages from market research to testing and implementation. This will allow the company to create more efficient and competitive products. The most important aspect of this strategy is the constant monitoring of competitors' activities and trend analysis, the company must have the ability to adapt quickly in a changing market. Peter  Draker  pays special attention to the concept of staff development and training, investments in their education and professional development as a guarantee of successful growth.      As mentioned above, the home perfumery market is highly competitive and fragmented, it is necessary to consider competitive strategies. The author of the theory of competitive strategies is Michael Porter, he proposed three main strategies: cost leadership,  differentiation  and focus.   The focus strategy has two variants, cost focus and differentiation focus. Cost leadership involves creating a product or service at the lowest price on the market, which allows the company to make more profit. To do this, it is necessary to optimize production processes, establish preferential relations with suppliers, reduce advertising and marketing costs, and use economies of scale. If the firm manages to reduce costs, it will be able to work above the average in its industry, while setting average industry prices. Cost leadership is suitable when cost advantage and broad targets take place. Differentiation is a strategy aimed at creating a product or service that differs from competitors and has unique characteristics. This may be the quality of the product, usability, or additional features. To do this, it is necessary to conduct market research and determine which characteristics are most important for consumers. This strategy is used with a broad target and product advantages. The focus strategy means focusing on a narrow market within the industry, the company chooses segments within the industry and focuses its attention on it, excluding others. This strategy has two options - cost focus and differentiation focus. The target focus should be buyers with unusual requests, in which case the company focuses all its efforts on this narrow group. In  order to build a competitive strategy, it is necessary to identify the strengths and weaknesses of the product, assess threats and opportunities  and,  based on them, make a decision on the vector of development, then develop a stable competitive advantage that will emphasize the chosen strategy and then identify key competitors and develop a tactical action plan to improve the competitiveness of the business.     One more tool to develop an effective strategy is The Balanced Scorecard, it is an organization management methodology that allows one to evaluate its activities from different angles and make the right strategic decisions. The system was developed in the 90s by Robert Kaplan, a professor at the Harvard University Business School, and David Norton, an American management consultant.  The basic principle of BSC is that an organization should have several key indicators that reflect its goals and objectives, as well as ways to achieve them. The first principle of BSC is to focus on the strategic goals of the organization. A balanced scorecard helps an organization identify its main goals and objectives, as well as select key indicators that reflect the achievement of these goals. At the same time, it should be borne in mind that the indicators should be measurable,  specific  and related to the final results. The second principle of BSC is the balance of indicators. An organization should have several categories of indicators that reflect different aspects of its activities: financial, client, internal  processes  and staff development. This approach allows you to evaluate the activities of the organization from different sides and make the right strategic decisions. The third principle of BSC is the relationship between indicators. Each indicator should be linked to other indicators and goals of the organization. For example, improving product quality can lead to an increase in customer satisfaction and, as a result, to an increase in profits. The fourth principle of BSC is continuous monitoring and analysis of indicators. The organization must constantly monitor its key indicators, analyze their  changes  and take measures to improve its performance. To successfully implement a balanced scorecard, several steps must be taken. Step 1. Defining the strategic goals of the organization. At this stage, it is necessary to determine the main goals and objectives of the organization, as well as select key indicators that reflect the achievement of these goals. Step 2. Develop indicators for each category. It is necessary to select indicators for each category: financial, client, internal  processes  and personnel development. Indicators should be measurable,  specific  and related to the final results. Step 3. Definition of target values for each indicator. It is necessary to define target values for each indicator that reflect the achievement of the strategic goals of the organization. Step 4. Develop an action plan. At this stage, it is necessary to develop an action plan to achieve the target values for each indicator. Step 5. Monitoring and analysis of indicators. The organization must constantly monitor its key indicators, analyze their  changes  and take measures to improve its performance. This process includes three main parts: strategic analysis, definition of goals and development of a strategic map. Strategic analysis is carried out to collect data on the external and internal environment of the company. PEST and SWOT methodologies are used for this. PEST analysis allows you to determine the impact of political, economic,  social  and technological factors on business. SWOT analysis helps to identify the strengths and weaknesses of the company, as well as opportunities and threats of the external environment. Based on the data obtained  as a result of  strategic analysis, the company's mission, vision, strategic goals and core values are formed. These elements become the basis for creating a corporate strategy and strategic map. The strategic map consists of four perspectives: financial, client, internal  processes  and personnel development. Key goals and indicators are defined for each perspective. For example, from a financial perspective, it may be an increase in profits or a reduction in costs. For the client perspective, it is an increase in customer satisfaction or an increase in market share. For internal processes – improving product quality or increasing production efficiency. For the perspective of personnel development – improving the system of training and development of employees or increasing motivation. At the last stage, specific goals and objectives are defined for each indicator, as well as target values that must be achieved. It is important to note that strategic planning is a continuous process that requires constant monitoring and analysis of indicators.      Another approach for strategy building described by James Moore in his book "The Death of Competition" . He offers a new approach to winning leadership based on the cooperation of the firm with its environment. He believes that competition should be viewed as a community  similar to  biological ecosystems, in which the integrity of the system is  impossible without cooperation between its members. Modern competition relies not only on rivalry, but also on cooperation. A firm claiming competitive leadership should strive to capture a dominant role in the ecosystem by implementing strategies that will ensure continued growth and continuous improvement of the ecosystem. A competitive advantage is that the company has an "ecological consciousness",  i.e.  knowledge about how to build ecosystems and how to manage them.      According to Philip Kotler and his book Fundamentals of Marketing, strategic planning is necessary to develop an expansion strategy. It is a managerial process of creating and maintaining a strategic correspondence between the goals of the company, its potential  opportunities  and chances in the field of marketing. It is based on a clearly formulated company policy statement, a statement of auxiliary goals and objectives, a healthy business portfolio and a growth strategy. Strategic planning includes four stages - the planning program, the tasks and goals of the company, the plans for the development of the economic portfolio and the growth strategy of the company. To determine the company's program, the owner needs to answer the questions: "What is our company? Who are our customers? What is valuable for these customers? What will our company be like? What should it be like?". A few years ago, Theodore Levitt stated that, in his opinion, the characteristics of an enterprise from the side of market activity are more accurate and more complete than its characteristics from a commodity or technological point of view.  He argues that an enterprise should be looked at as a customer's needs and requests, and not as a manufacturer of certain goods. Goods and technologies eventually become obsolete, while the basic needs and demands of the market can remain unchanged forever. When developing a market-oriented policy statement, management should strive to ensure that the program does not turn out to be too narrow or too broad. The development of the program is necessary not only for the owner, but also for employees who, after reading a well-described program, will be able to feel part of a large-scale business, as well as for clients who will see in the company not just a money-making enterprise, but the soul and DNA. When developing a policy statement, management should define the scope of the company's activities in terms of meeting customer needs, and not just the production of goods or the use of technology.  To effectively manage the company's program, it is necessary to develop a  detailed list of auxiliary goals and objectives  for each level of management. Each manager should have his own tasks for which he is responsible, which is called problem solving management. Profits can be increased by increasing sales growth, reducing costs, or using both strategies. To increase the share of the domestic market, the firm must make its goods more affordable and use more intensive  incentives, and to enter foreign markets - to reduce prices and concentrate on large farms. Each marketing strategy should be described in detail, including increasing the number of sellers and intensifying advertising. Tasks should be turned into specific goals that can be measured in time and magnitude. Managers use the term "goal" to denote tasks that are specified in terms of magnitude and time, which facilitates planning and control. Having studied the dangers and opportunities associated with the product, the manager is able to set tasks and outline the range of problems that arise in this case. The objectives should be formulated in the form of goals that the firm seeks to achieve during the period of the plan. The last stage is considered a marketing strategy - a rational, logical construction, guided by which an organizational unit expects to solve its marketing tasks. It includes specific strategies for target markets, the marketing  mix  and the level of marketing costs.  The marketing strategy should accurately name the market segments on which the firm will focus its main efforts. These segments differ from each other in terms of preference,  response  and profitability. The firm will act very wisely by concentrating efforts and energy on the segments that it can serve best from a competitive point of view. A separate marketing strategy should be developed for each of the selected target segments. The manager should outline specific strategies for such elements of the marketing mix as new products, organization of local sales, advertising, sales promotion,  prices  and distribution of goods.     After analyzing the above theories about expansion strategy, it became clear that  in order to  build a growth strategy, innovations are needed, which, according to Peter Drucker, are born from constant monitoring of trends, observing a competitor and understanding the needs of their consumers. However, all this should be done in accordance with the global goals of the company, based on the mission and values.  Consequently, the  following steps are proposed: 1. Strategic planning. This stage includes identifying the strategic direction of the firm, its mission,  vision,  and values through a deep interview with the founder. This part also includes analysis of resources with the usage of VRIO analysis to identify sustainable ones. 2. Analysis of market. This part includes analysis of the external environment with the help of PESTEL analysis, which identifies opportunities and threats for the company growth. It also includes identification of potential demand of new defined segments of customers with the help of offline short interviews with target audience and analysis of potential demand among Russian consumers with the help of online surveys. To analyze competitors, benchmarking and KSF analysis will be used.  3. Development recommendations for the expansion strategy. 1.4 Data source      The following data collection tools will be used  –  a  deep  interview with the owner of the company, to understand the values and plans of the company,  in order to  subsequently formulate a strategic mission and vision , questions will concern about goals of organization, desired results and uniqueness of the company.  Offline surveys  with foreign customers of Hermitage Museum shop will be conducted  in order to  identify potential demand for candles with paintings on the label as a souvenir from Saint Petersburg or simple gift. Research among Russian target customers  will be conducted  in order to  analyze consumer preferences and to define the relevance of a brand's unique selling proposition.    Information from  competitor’s  websites and social networks will be used to conduct competitor analysis.  Secondary research (rating agencies, previously conducted research), own experience and observations, social  networks,  and forums to formulate strategic recommendations.  Part  2.  Strategic  planning 2.1. Mission,  vision  and values     According to the BSC system, strategic planning begins with understanding what a company is, what its mission is and for whom this company exists. The first stage is to determine the main parameters of the strategy - mission,  values  and vision. A mission statement communicates the organization’s reasons for being, and how it serves its key stakeholders. Peter Drucker  advises to understand the purpose of the organization - the mission - to answer yourself five questions: What are the goals of our organization? Who was it created for (who are its services intended for)? What is unique about our programs (projects)? What will be our results? What is your plan of action? The answers to these questions allow managers to clearly understand what their organization exists for, find out strengths and weaknesses, and determine the scope of work.     From the interview with the founder of the company the goal of the company is to popularize art in daily life. The company believes that the integration of art objects into everyday life will give more opportunities for inspiration and  getting  joys  and pleasure from simple things. In general, art is an integral part of life and can have a profound impact on psychological and emotional life. Therefore, it is important not to forget about it and give it enough space in our daily life. As for the financial goals, they are ambitious, as can be seen from the above data on the financial statements of market players with a similar business model - there is money and demand in the home fragrances market, the question is to win competition and competently build an expansion strategy. In the short term, there is an urgent need to expand the product line (for example, diffusers, bath bombs, etc.) and build a competent marketing strategy to attract new customers, in the long term, the brand wants to become an analogue of HM home/ ZARA home.       Turning to the second question, scented candles are a mass product that is bought mainly by women. They are suitable for people who like to create comfort in the house and decorate the interior. The collection was created from high-quality raw materials and very expensive premium fragrances, so this brand is for connoisseurs who understand niche perfumes. The  brand even had reviews that candles smell like Dior.  Taking into account  the ART direction, we can also say that the brand was created for people who appreciate beauty and harmony in the environment, sincerely love fragrances and know how to enjoy the moment, who know how to enjoy life. For them, scented candles are not just a decorative item or a means to create an atmosphere, they are a way to express their taste and mood, to create coziness and comfort in their home. Fromm brand candles were created as a functional and aesthetic element of the interior. The uniqueness of the brand lies in its positioning and in its ingredients. Most competitors in the market use the same perfumes, the same containers, and almost the same minimalistic design, do not broadcast a personal brand and have no values. Fromm ventured away from minimalism in the opposite direction and did not lose, most partners are delighted with the brand thanks to its art collection, and the trend for art has not faded for several millennia. The founder of the brand believes that Russia and the world will like it. As a result of constant development and growth, the brand will expand, to begin with, expand its product line, expand the geography of partners in Russia and the world and increase its awareness around the world. To effectively build a growth strategy of the company, it is necessary to conduct a comparative analysis of competitors with a similar business model and study the practices of international and Russian companies.  The study of international business practices can be a valuable tool for developing effective business strategies. By understanding how other companies have successfully expanded into new markets, businesses can gain valuable insights that can help them develop their own strategies. Studying the practices of international companies can help businesses get ideas for new products and services. International companies are always looking for new ways to grow their businesses, and by studying their practices, businesses can get ideas for new products and services that they can offer their customers. It can help to learn about different marketing and sales techniques. International companies have a lot of experience in marketing and selling their products in different markets.  Moreover  it can provide insights into the challenges and opportunities that it will face, and it will help the business develop a strategy that is more likely to be successful.     Benchmarking will be used as a tool to conduct comparative analysis of competitors. Benchmarking is a process of comparing a business to other businesses in the industry to identify best practices. It can be a valuable tool for developing effective business strategies, especially for businesses that are considering expanding into new markets. There are  a number of  reasons why benchmarking can be useful for studying the practices of international companies. First, it can help businesses identify the best practices that are used in different markets. This can help businesses develop strategies that are more likely to be  successful in those markets. Second, benchmarking can help businesses identify the gaps between their own practices and the best practices in their industry. This can help businesses identify areas where they need to improve their practices  in order to  be more competitive. Third, benchmarking can help businesses identify the factors that contribute to the success of other businesses. This can help businesses develop strategies that are more likely to be successful.  Experts interviewed by RBC Trends say that benchmarking is used today by companies in solving any business problem. At the same time, it is noted that an important condition for benchmarking is qualitative data. Many Chinese tourists use organized tours when visiting Russia, so these partnerships can provide direct access to the target market. As stated in the research , most Chinese tourists prefer 3 or 4 star hotels, therefore, in order to increase sales and brand awareness, it is possible to arrange the placement of advertising booklets and/ or products in such hotels as: "House at the Hermitage", "Venice", "Faces", "Hotel Apartments YES" , so as they are the closest in values and positioning to the Fromm brand. Based on the strategic recommendations business  canva  model should be represented as follows: Conclusion      This final qualifying work was devoted to the development of practical recommendations on the expansion strategy of the Fromm company to increase its sales in the market. Fromm is a company engaged in the production and sale of scented candles. Currently, the company is faced with the problem of high competition in the market and slow revenue growth, as well as poor brand awareness.    In the first step was identified strategic goals, then mission, vision and values were formulated. With the help of VRIO analysis were defined sustainable resources of the company and competitive advantage form was chosen. The next step was market analysis with identification of external factors that affects company’s operations. Based on that analysis new customer segment was found and further research was made on this hypothesis. Moreover, competitors’ analysis was made in form of benchmarking, which help to identify current trend in the industry, such as minimalistic design of the packaging and natural fragrances. It also helped to identify relevant ways of promotion and how brands gain new markets. Analysis showed, that even though candles are needed to be touched and smelled before the purchase, many firms successfully sell it online.       In order to  increase the company's sales and brand awareness, practical recommendations on the company's strategy were developed during the writing of this work, taking into account both the size of the company and the specifics of the market. The main recommendations were the differentiation of the product line with the help of new products, such as kitchen and dining accessories, as well as stationery, as well as the development of the company's marketing strategy focused on promotion through social networks and  microinflencers , an important aspect is also the expansion of the network of partners and the creation of collaborations for the exchange of audiences, which ultimately will lead to increase in sales and profits of the company.
